api/v3/handlers/subscriptions/subscripti.purpose: v3 HTTP handlers for subscription addons (list and get); implements the httptransport.HandlerWithArgs pipeline converting between domain subscriptionaddon.SubscriptionAddon and apiv3.SubscriptionAddon types.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs decode/operate/encode pipeline
patterns.description: Every endpoint uses httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs with three functions: decode params+request, call the domain service, encode response. Never write a raw http.Handler.

patterns.name: Namespace resolution via injected closure
patterns.description: Namespace is resolved by calling h.resolveNamespace(ctx) inside the decode function — never parsed from URL params directly.

patterns.name: Pagination via pagination.NewPage with validation
patterns.description: Page input is built via pagination.NewPage(number, size) with defaults (1, 20), then validated with page.Validate() before building the service input.

patterns.name: Error encoding via apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder
patterns.description: List appends apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() as the error encoder. Never use commonhttp.GenericErrorEncoder() in v3 handlers.

patterns.name: Sort parsing via request.ParseSortBy + OrderBy.Validate
patterns.description: Sort params parse with request.ParseSortBy, map to subscriptionaddon.OrderBy, set Order via sort.Order.ToSortxOrder(), then validate with OrderBy.Validate(); bad fields return 400.
patterns.example: input.OrderBy = subscriptionaddon.OrderBy(sort.Field); if err := input.OrderBy.Validate(); err != nil { return ..., apierrors.NewBadRequestError(...) }

patterns.name: Conversion in convert.go only
patterns.description: All domain-to-API conversion is in convert.go as standalone functions (toAPISubscriptionAddon). Operate functions call them; conversion is never embedded in list.go/get.go.

patterns.name: Handler interface + struct + per-file endpoints
patterns.description: handler.go defines the Handler interface (ListSubscriptionAddons, GetSubscriptionAddon), a private handler struct (resolveNamespace, addonService, options), and New(). Each endpoint lives in its own file.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { ListSubscriptionAddons() ListSubscriptionAddonsHandler; GetSubscriptionAddon() GetSubscriptionAddonHandler }

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface, handler struct (resolveNamespace, addonService, options), New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding dependencies requires wiring in api/v3/server/server.go. Keep struct fields minimal.

key_file_guides.role: ListSubscriptionAddons: typed params, request, response; pagination + sort validation; per-item toAPISubscriptionAddon mapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Apply default page (1, 20) before Validate(). Unknown sort fields must return 400 via OrderBy.Validate(), not 500.

key_file_guides.role: GetSubscriptionAddon: builds GetSubscriptionAddonInput (NamespacedID + SubscriptionID); returns 404 when the service result is nil.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Note get.go does NOT append apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder in its options block (only WithOperationName) — list.go does.

key_file_guides.role: toAPISubscriptionAddon: reads clock.Now() to pick the active instance, unions all instance periods into one ActiveFrom/ActiveTo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns NewGenericNotFoundError if no instance is active at now or no instances exist — callers must handle it. Union uses lo.Reduce; new fields must stay in sync with apiv3.SubscriptionAddon.

anti_patterns: Calling addonService methods from the decode function — decode only parses/validates inputs.
Using commonhttp.GenericErrorEncoder() in v3 handlers; always use apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder().
Embedding domain-to-API conversion inline in list.go/get.go — put it in convert.go.
Skipping page.Validate() before using the page value.
Resolving namespace from URL path params directly instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx).

decisions.decision: toAPISubscriptionAddon reads clock.Now() and unions all instance periods into a single ActiveFrom/ActiveTo with a current quantity snapshot.
decisions.rationale: The domain stores multiple timed instances; the API exposes a single flattened addon view with a full effective range.

decisions.decision: Sort validation uses subscriptionaddon.OrderBy.Validate() after field mapping.
decisions.rationale: Keeps supported sort fields authoritative in the domain package; the handler does not hardcode valid field names.

code_examples.scenario: Converting a domain addon with multiple instances to the flattened API view

api/v3/handlers/taxcodes.purpose: Full CRUD v3 HTTP handler package for billing tax codes (list/get/create/upsert/delete) plus organization-default tax-code management. Uses Goverter-generated conversions driven by annotated convert.go and a backwards-compatible AppType mapping.

patterns.name: Goverter variables pattern for conversions
patterns.description: convert.go declares package-level var function fields with goverter directives; convert.gen.go is generated (DO NOT EDIT) and assigns them in init(). The //go:generate directive at the top of convert.go regenerates via 'make generate'.
patterns.example:
// goverter:variables
var FromAPICreateTaxCodeRequest func(namespace string, req api.CreateTaxCodeRequest) (taxcode.CreateTaxCodeInput, error)

patterns.name: Backwards-compatible AppType mapping
patterns.description: FromAPIBillingAppType maps API 'external_invoicing' to app.AppTypeCustomInvoicing; ToAPIBillingAppType maps back to 'external_invoicing'. The pair must stay in sync.
patterns.example: func FromAPIBillingAppType(s api.BillingAppType) app.AppType { if s == "external_invoicing" { return app.AppTypeCustomInvoicing }; return app.AppType(s) }

patterns.name: Nil-to-empty-slice in app mappings
patterns.description: ToAPIBillingTaxCodeAppMappings returns []api.BillingTaxCodeAppMapping{} (not nil) when the source is nil, preventing a JSON null on the AppMappings field.
patterns.example: func ToAPIBillingTaxCodeAppMappings(s taxcode.TaxCodeAppMappings) []api.BillingTaxCodeAppMapping { if s == nil { return []api.BillingTaxCodeAppMapping{} }; ... }

patterns.name: Goverter context for namespace/namespacedID injection
patterns.description: goverter:context + goverter:map ... | NamespaceFromContext / ResolveNamespacedIDFromContext inject the namespace/NamespacedID into generated converters from a context argument.

patterns.name: Upsert semantics for the update endpoint
patterns.description: update.go accepts api.UpsertTaxCodeRequest (not UpdateTaxCodeRequest) and uses operation name 'upsert-tax-code' — the endpoint is an upsert, not a partial update.

key_file_guides.role: Source-of-truth for Goverter declarations: //go:generate directive, goverter:variables/extend block, var function fields, and hand-written helpers (AppType mapping, nil-safe app mappings, tax-code reference <-> ID).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Editing annotations requires 'make generate'. A new goverter:extend helper must also be registered in the top goverter:extend block.

key_file_guides.role: Goverter-generated implementations assigning the var fields in init(); carries the DO NOT EDIT header.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never edit directly — regenerate with 'make generate'. Out-of-sync conversions produce silently incorrect mappings.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (7 methods incl. organization-default tax codes) and handler struct with resolveNamespace + taxcode.Service only.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No secondary service — taxcode.Service handles all CRUD and organization defaults.

key_file_guides.role: Upsert via api.UpsertTaxCodeRequest; passes a models.NamespacedID to FromAPIUpsertTaxCodeRequest.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Operation name must be 'upsert-tax-code'. Using api.UpdateTaxCodeRequest breaks JSON deserialization.

key_file_guides.role: Org-default endpoints using bare httptransport.NewHandler (no path arg); reference IDs convert via the *ReferenceToIDString helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Reference helpers (InvoicingTaxCodeReferenceToIDString etc.) return NewGenericValidationError when id is missing — required-field enforcement.

key_file_guides.role: Standard list/create/get/delete; conversions via ToAPIBillingTaxCode and FromAPICreateTaxCodeRequest.
key_file_guides.watch_for: delete.go defines DeleteTaxCodeRequest as a non-alias struct (taxcode.DeleteTaxCodeInput, no =) and rebuilds the input before the service call.

anti_patterns: Editing convert.gen.go directly — regenerate via 'make generate' after changing goverter annotations.
Breaking the AppType round-trip — 'external_invoicing' must map both ways through FromAPIBillingAppType and ToAPIBillingAppType.
Returning nil for AppMappings instead of an empty slice — causes a JSON null that breaks the array contract.
Adding a goverter:extend helper without registering it in the top goverter:extend block.
Using api.UpdateTaxCodeRequest in update.go instead of api.UpsertTaxCodeRequest.

decisions.decision: Goverter for tax-code conversions instead of hand-written mappers.
decisions.rationale: Tax codes are straightforward field mapping with no discriminated unions; Goverter cuts boilerplate while context injects namespace cleanly.
The domain type was renamed from external_invoicing to custom_invoicing; the API preserves the old name for backwards compatibility.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new Goverter conversion variable following the existing pattern

api/v3/labels.purpose: Bridges the api.Labels wire type (map[string]string) to domain models.Metadata and models.Annotations, enforcing key/value format rules and the 'openmeter_' annotation prefix convention. All v3 endpoints accepting or returning labels route through here.

patterns.name: ToMetadataAnnotations for inbound labels
patterns.description: Splits keys into Metadata (user keys) and Annotations (openmeter_-prefixed, prefix stripped). Validates every key/value; collects errors via errors.Join and wraps with NewNillableGenericValidationError.
patterns.example: ma, err := labels.ToMetadataAnnotations(req.Body.Labels); if err != nil { return err }; input.Metadata = ma.Metadata

patterns.name: FromMetadataAnnotations for outbound labels
patterns.description: Builds *api.Labels from domain Metadata + Annotations. Annotation keys are re-prefixed with 'openmeter_'; annotation values that are not string/Stringer/TextMarshaler are silently skipped, as are keys failing ValidateLabel.

patterns.name: ValidateLabel / ValidateLabels for format + reserved-prefix checks
patterns.description: Validates against keyValueFormat regexp and reservedPrefixMatcher; returns models.ValidationIssue errors carrying WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(400) so they render as 400 through the error encoder.
patterns.example: if err := labels.ValidateLabels(*req.Labels); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: openmeter_ prefix as the annotation namespace
patterns.description: API keys starting with 'openmeter_' map to models.Annotations (prefix stripped); all others map to models.Metadata. User-supplied 'openmeter_' keys are rejected to prevent spoofing internal annotations.

patterns.name: FromMetadata / ToMetadata convenience helpers
patterns.description: FromMetadata[T ~map[string]string] and ToMetadata wrap the annotation-aware converters for entities that only carry Metadata (no Annotations).
patterns.example: func ToMetadata(labels *api.Labels) (models.Metadata, error) { m, err := ToMetadataAnnotations(labels); return m.Metadata, err }

key_file_guides.role: ToMetadataAnnotations (inbound) and FromMetadataAnnotations (outbound) plus FromMetadata/ToMetadata helpers; defines AnnotationsPrefix = "openmeter_".
key_file_guides.watch_for: FromMetadataAnnotations handles Stringer, TextMarshaler, and string annotation values; other types (and failing MarshalText) are silently skipped with no error.

key_file_guides.role: ValidateLabel / ValidateLabels using compiled keyValueFormat and reservedPrefixMatcher regexps; issues carry WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(400).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Reserved prefixes include openmeter, kong, konnect, insomnia, mesh, kic, kuma, and a leading underscore — user keys with these are always rejected, even outside annotation contexts.

anti_patterns: Accepting api.Labels in a domain service input — domain types use models.Metadata and models.Annotations.
Skipping ValidateLabel when writing annotation keys from external input — invalid keys silently disappear in FromMetadataAnnotations.
Checking the 'openmeter_' prefix directly in handler code instead of calling ToMetadataAnnotations.
Relying on annotation values that are not string/Stringer/TextMarshaler — they are silently dropped on output.

decisions.decision: The openmeter_ prefix splits labels into metadata vs annotations at the API boundary.
decisions.rationale: Domain code uses typed Annotations (any values) while the wire type is map[string]string; the prefix acts as a namespace marker surviving the round-trip without a separate wire field.

code_examples.scenario: Converting inbound labels in a create handler and outbound in a response mapper
